question: What does the Health & self-audit admin page do?
expectedAnswer: It runs a self-audit probing the Console UAMI's identity, Cosmos/Synapse/ADX/AOAI reachability, RBAC grants, and security posture, reporting each as pass/warn/fail with the exact remediation — answering whether the deployment is wired correctly right now.

question: What is the service exercise on the health page?
expectedAnswer: /api/admin/health/exercise drives every backend once with a real round-trip per service, so green means genuinely working, not merely configured.

question: How does one-click heal work and is it safe?
expectedAnswer: For fixable findings (a missing role grant, an empty env var) the healer applies the fix only on explicit admin approval, audits it, and re-checks; findings it can't safely fix show the manual step instead of being auto-applied.

question: What does the deep health endpoint probe?
expectedAnswer: /api/health/deep runs bounded reachability probes — for example Cosmos getDatabaseAccount — while the exercise route does full data-plane round-trips per service.

question: What scope does the Loom Monitor surface cover?
expectedAnswer: Everything CSA Loom deployed — the Azure resources in the Loom resource groups (Container Apps, Cosmos, AI Search, ADX, Synapse/ADF, APIM, Foundry/AOAI, App Insights) plus the Cosmos-backed item telemetry of who deployed and edited what.

question: How does Monitor show resource health?
expectedAnswer: The Overview tab shows a health badge per resource with roll-up stats from the real Microsoft.ResourceHealth availabilityStatuses REST API.

question: How does the Metrics tab work?
expectedAnswer: Per-resource SVG time-series charts with time-range and refresh, from the real microsoft.insights/metrics REST, using a curated metric catalog keyed by resource type (CPU/mem/requests, Cosmos RU, AI Search QPS, ADX CPU, ADF runs, AOAI tokens).

question: Can I run ad-hoc KQL against logs from Monitor?
expectedAnswer: Yes — the Logs tab has an ad-hoc KQL editor, result grid, five curated presets, and a time-range, POSTing to the Log Analytics query API. If LOOM_LOG_ANALYTICS_WORKSPACE_ID is unset an honest gate names it.

question: What does the Activity log tab show?
expectedAnswer: Control-plane events (time/operation/status/RG/caller) from the Microsoft.Insights management events REST, with a 24h-90d window filter scoped to the Loom resource groups.

question: Can I author alert rules from the Monitor surface?
expectedAnswer: The Alerts tab lists metric-alert rules (name/enabled/severity/RG) from the metricAlerts REST; rule authoring is an honest not-yet-wired note — manage rules in the portal until the PUT path lands.

question: Which role grants does the Monitor surface need?
expectedAnswer: Monitoring Reader on the Loom subscription (metrics, activity log, alerts, resource health) and Log Analytics Reader on the workspace (KQL queries), held by the Console UAMI.

question: What happens on the Monitor page when LOOM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID is unset?
expectedAnswer: The full UI still renders with an honest warning MessageBar: inventory/metrics/activity/alerts gate on LOOM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID / LOOM_ADMIN_RG, and Logs gates on LOOM_LOG_ANALYTICS_WORKSPACE_ID.

question: Where does the Deployed items tab get its data?
expectedAnswer: From the Cosmos audit-log plus comments and shares via /api/activity — the existing activity feed showing who ran, edited, or shared each deployed item.

question: Is there a Gov override for the Log Analytics endpoint?
expectedAnswer: Yes — LOOM_LOG_ANALYTICS_ENDPOINT is optional and defaults to https://api.loganalytics.azure.com; set it for the Gov endpoint.

question: How is the Monitor backend implemented and authenticated?
expectedAnswer: lib/azure/monitor-client.ts holds the Azure REST callers and metric catalog, authenticated with the UAMI-first chained credential like every other Loom ARM client; BFF routes are session-validated and return structured ok/data/error JSON with honest gates.
